title: Chapter 406 – Peerless Realm (6)
content:
Jeong Yeon-shin focused his gaze.

The forest wind lashed fiercely against his face. The purple robe fastened at both shoulders somehow refused to fall, only billowing wide behind him.

Upon the white line that cut mysteriously through the void.

Jeong Yeon-shin was running along it like an acrobat on a tightrope. His lightness skill was faster than ever before, and still accelerating.

Whoosh! Whoosh! Whoooosh--!

The air crumpled beneath his feet.

His strides were enormously long.

Wherever his soles touched down, ripples spread as though he were stepping on water, and the circular shockwaves that bloomed in his wake fell far behind him almost instantly.

Lightness skill: Ten Li Gentle Light.

As he raced along the beam of the Shooting Moon Single Path, his vision blurred beyond recognition.

This was a speed he could scarcely reach under normal circumstances. Even with the Lowly Revelation Technique active, the forest scenery was little more than a smear of color.

'I was lucky.'

It was the same as when he had faced Namgung Mu-jin.

Just as he had used the Dark Night Palace Leader's Transcendent Martial Art to shatter Namgung Mu-jin's, the propulsive energy waves now surging beneath his feet were also powered by that stolen technique.

"Something's coming. Wait--is that a person?"

"Above! No, behind!"

"Void treading...?"

"It's an enemy!"

Commotion erupted below.

The masters forming the dragnet. Those who whipped their heads toward Jeong Yeon-shin--this figure who appeared to be running through the sky--grew steadily in number.

More than a few tried to hurl spears or daggers before giving up.

By the time he entered their vision, Jeong Yeon-shin had already vanished into the distance.

'There are so many. Is this Mount Huaguo?'

He was struck anew by the sheer vastness of the dragnet arrayed against the Divine Sword Group Leader.

The scenery below brushed past his eyes in faint, continuous flashes.

Martial artists concealed in ambush along seemingly empty paths. A dozen swordsmen sitting cross-legged in formation with blades laid across their knees. Ronin disguised as common villagers in crudely built settlements.

Every place his gaze touched was teeming with people.

They said one individual had personally overseen this entire dragnet.

'Yu Ling Leader...'

A peerless master reputed to possess deeply sinister skills.

How many others in the martial world could mobilize such power?

The Yu Ling Leader rarely drew his sword unless victory was assured. There wasn't even a portrait depicting his face.

Word was that the High Sword of Wudang had given chase, but the odds of catching him were slim.

Capturing a peerless master determined to hide or flee was nearly impossible.

Even Venerable Cypress Flame of Emei--whose martial depth Jeong Yeon-shin couldn't fathom at a glance--had ultimately failed to capture the Pure Demon Alliance Leader.

Likewise, the Qingcheng Sect Leader had quickly abandoned pursuit to protect civilian homes, only to encounter the Golden Wing Sect Leader after descending from Yunhui Peak.

A peerless master intent on escape was a truly elusive creature.

The Dark Night Palace Leader was no different.

The world's finest archer had ceased supporting Namgung Mu-jin at some point--likely to conserve his strength.

A temperament that prioritized self-preservation above all else. Since he had almost certainly been contemplating escape even then, the only clue for tracking him was the trace of Transcendent Martial Art that Jeong Yeon-shin was now treading upon.

Screeeech--!

It came from above his head. The cry of some bird echoed loudly, reverberating through the sky. Its hostility was unmistakable.

Jeong Yeon-shin recognized the creature without looking up.

The grotesque bird that had guided him to this land through the Young Palace Leader of Dark Night Palace. A famous mystical creature, even revered as sacred by the old noble clans.

'Dangerous.'

Jeong Yeon-shin drew the fragments of the Luminous Wheel from his heart to his skin. The instant his entire body was enveloped in a faint nimbus of light--

Bang--!

His right shoulder was wrenched violently backward.

An arrow had struck without warning. The impact traveled from shoulder to chest. He had clearly defended with protective energy, yet the force still reached him.

The arrow that had failed to penetrate his internal energy barrier bounced away past his shoulder.

Jeong Yeon-shin's eyes sharpened. He was still racing along the faintly glowing thread of the Shooting Moon Single Path.

'Dark Night Palace Leader!'

The arrow had been imbued with highly refined internal weight technique.

This was a skill that would earn recognition as a master even when deployed through fist techniques, yet the black arrowhead had smoothly bypassed his protective energy and detonated its force upon impact.

It had reached its mark.

He had entered the Dark Night Palace Leader's medium-range combat zone. Normally, the energy within an arrow would dissipate greatly over long distances, but not this time.

Dodging the Ten Li Instant Kill from far away would have been far easier.

'Again...!'

Jeong Yeon-shin dropped low.

In the same instant, an arrow grazed his ear.

An explosive roar like cannon fire--whoooong--tore through the atmosphere, and his sideburns were yanked upward as if sucked into the arrow's wake.

It was purely luck.

The Dark Night Palace Leader's arrows had grown even faster, and combined with the speed of the Ten Li Gentle Light, evading a single shot was nearly miraculous.

'I must protect my head.'

His opponent possessed archery that could ignore even divine-level protective energy.

A hit near his ear from the internal weight technique of a peerless master would make it impossible to maintain balance.

Jeong Yeon-shin was not yet the foremost swordsman in the martial world.

But the Dark Night Palace Leader was universally acknowledged as the World's Finest Bow. That was what the martial world called him.

Therefore.

Crack.

The toe of Jeong Yeon-shin's foot, which had been gliding along the faint thread, twisted slightly outward.

Instantly, a heavy weight settled into his thigh.

At his speed, the motion defied reason, but his bundle of tendons was strong enough to endure even this. His bone transformation had been structured that way from the very beginning.

He could overcome the weakness of the Lightning Genius martial lineage's straight-line trajectories through variation. This was possible because he was born with a transparent temperament like that of a gentleman--though he had never properly used variations until now.

'Now...!'

A transparent shockwave erupted from the front of his ankle where the Earth Rushing acupoint lay.

Splat!

His form, which had been shooting forward in a straight line, dissolved into a blur.

In that instant, a black arrow passed clean through the space where his body had dispersed like mist. The air was violently torn apart.

And then--Jeong Yeon-shin, somehow rematerialized, had a gleam of interest in his eyes.

'This is the Burning Abyss Squad's variation...'

He had diverted some of the propulsive energy waves he normally used solely for acceleration. The principle was simple enough. He had dropped his body and rotated.

By briefly cutting his speed and then surging back to a sprint, the entire maneuver had occurred in a flash.

How would it have appeared to his opponent?

The Dark Night Palace Leader was said to possess eye techniques befitting his title as the World's Finest Bow.

He must still be tracking Jeong Yeon-shin clearly. If their positions were reversed, Jeong Yeon-shin would be furious.

A hint of satisfaction crossed the young man's eyes.

In truth, the Dark Night Palace Leader's arrows had struck him more often than Namgung Mu-jin's sword.

Was it the difference between a second-in-command of the Thirteen Evil Sects and a leader? From the moment the Dark Night Palace Leader had begun to flee, a quiet anger had been building in Jeong Yeon-shin's chest.

Today, by whatever means necessary, he would slay the World's Finest Bow under the banner of Desolate Fortress' Divine Sword Group.

'With righteous cause.'

Swaaaang--!

Three black arrows came flying at once.

The archery was so fierce it left three colorless trails scored through the void, yet only a single sound--bang--came from Jeong Yeon-shin's body as it dispersed once more into a hazy afterimage.

One arrow fell with its tip crushed.

The two that had narrowly grazed both sides of his body streaked away with tremendous force.

Jeong Yeon-shin exhaled a long breath. The impact filled his split abdominal muscles like lightning and shook his internal organs. The bow energy grew stronger with each volley.

Even though the Dark Night Palace Leader's reserves must have been greatly depleted by the Transcendent Martial Art.

The distance had narrowed. Of that, he was certain.

'I'm almost there.'

Jeong Yeon-shin did not slow his sprint.

He pressed on, repeatedly blurring and resolidifying as though employing the legendary earth-shrinking technique. He had fully adapted to the Shooting Moon Single Path's white line--which was itself a transcendent marvel.

How long had he been running?

Even though it was a season of long days, twilight had already fallen. By the time the reddish glow of sunset draped itself over his long, streaming purple robe--

After absorbing thirteen of sixty-seven arrows with his body, Jeong Yeon-shin's gaze had grown razor-sharp.

In the distance.

He could make out someone's back, small but unmistakable.

The moment Jeong Yeon-shin locked onto him, the distance collapsed.

A figure treading across tree branches as though they were level ground. Faded white hair, a long black robe billowing behind him, an antique red sandalwood chest strapped to his back, and a great bow held in one hand--the sight was striking.

Fire-Quelling Grand Demon Bow.

The red wooden bow was curved slightly on the diagonal. Though it appeared simple at first glance, it was the symbol of the World's Finest Bowman--as famous as the Dog-Beating Staff of the Beggar Sect Leader, whose lineage stretched back centuries.

--Remarkable.

A rough, low voice reached Jeong Yeon-shin's ear.

Their gazes met.

The Dark Night Palace Leader, his ashen eyes glinting, moved amid fluttering afterimages that trembled like moth wings.

He had already nocked an arrow to the Fire-Quelling Grand Demon Bow and twisted just his upper body. The arrow was aimed squarely at Jeong Yeon-shin.

--Though you have arrived far sooner than I expected, would you believe me if I said this was a lure?

The Dark Night Palace Leader drew his bowstring.

A creaking sound reached Jeong Yeon-shin's ears. The tension was immense. The spiral vortex streaming from the arrow's fletching scraped roughly against the ground below.

Woong--

In that instant, the faint white line of the Shooting Moon Single Path that Jeong Yeon-shin had been treading rippled once, then sank into the arrowhead.

Jeong Yeon-shin, now forced to tread on tree branches.

The Dark Night Palace Leader's eyes seemed to say: If you had been satisfied with slaying one man after withstanding the combined assault of two peerless masters, that would have been enough.

At this moment, Jeong Yeon-shin's protective energy was threadbare.

He had pursued after fighting battle upon battle. The next arrow might punch clean through his body.

The air was drawn tight as a bowstring.

"......"

Both men ceased their sprints. They descended onto separate tree branches, dozens of zhang apart.

Their gazes locked through the vortex swirling at the tip of the Dark Night Palace Leader's arrow.

Peerless masters sizing each other up.

Both were far from peak condition. Their weakened energy was so obvious that neither could hide it from the other.

"No major faction can remain intact after losing a martial artist of your caliber. Your death will be the beginning of Desolate Fortress' annihilation."

The Dark Night Palace Leader spoke in an archaic manner, as befitted one who had lived many years as a member of the old nobility.

The World's Finest Bow, said to possess unparalleled concentration in all the martial world.

The absence of emotion in his eyes suggested he had thoroughly assessed Jeong Yeon-shin's depleted state.

"Do you feel no shame? Engaging in a combined attack."

When Jeong Yeon-shin asked this quietly, a faint curve formed on the Dark Night Palace Leader's lips.

"If you seek the honor of combat between martial artists, take it up with the Heaven Conquering Sect Leader. For me, martial arts is merely a cornerstone for rebuilding a fallen nation, and the warrior's code is simply trivial. Your Desolate Fortress is no different. Does a constable discuss fairness when arresting a criminal? I have merely returned the favor."

"A black path scoundrel. At least you know what you are."

"...I pay my respects to the martial prowess that broke through my interference and slew Namgung First Sword. As a martial artist, I have lost."

Then, without warning--

The Dark Night Palace Leader casually released his bowstring. With that simple gesture, the air made a tearing sound--jjaaaack.

The ultimate technique of a Thirteen Evil Sects leader. He clearly intended to kill. Even as the arrow flew, the Dark Night Palace Leader was already drawing the next shaft from the chest on his back.

All of it appeared in slow motion to Jeong Yeon-shin.

A life-or-death duel decided in a single exchange, an arrow hurtling toward the space between his brows--

'Ah.'

Jeong Yeon-shin realized suddenly that the Dark Night Palace Leader was just as tense as he was. Neither had looked up, so intent were they on each other.

Suddenly.

There was a presence dropping between them.

An intruder.

The energy control was exquisitely refined. Had they been waiting, watching for an opening? Energy waves that had been completely suppressed were unleashed in an instant, forged in a flash into the shape of a sword. A sinister vibration radiated from the blade of internal energy conjured by the black-robed intruder.

It was closing in fast.

The rustle of garments was elegant.

Crack--

There was no time to hesitate.

Jeong Yeon-shin's foot kicked off the tree branch. His body shot forward in a straight line amid fragments of wood shattered by propulsive energy.

Screeeeeech!

The instant Jeong Yeon-shin activated the Demonic Blood Circulation Technique and reached upward, the Blood Guardian Squad's supreme ultimate technique--Dark Night Radiant Blood Demon Sword--was in his hand.

A dark red mist shaped like a sword.

It appeared while Jeong Yeon-shin's form was still distorted in the void. It was the support of Blood Extreme Demon Jin Myeong-jo, placed precisely in his trajectory.

Jeong Yeon-shin slashed through the space with a single stroke.

"...!"

A crimson line was carved across the void. A trajectory that cut horizontally through the black arrow and passed through the Dark Night Palace Leader's neck, flashing as a single scarlet streak. The delayed backwind knocked the World's Finest Bow's head clean off.

The thud that followed was desolate.

The Dark Night Palace Leader's head rolled across the dirt, then stared up at the sky with bulging eyes. His face was frozen in shock and disbelief.

Moments later, his body lost its balance and collapsed on top of it with a heavy thump.

Saaaa--

Early summer exhaled a dry breath.

The wind turned gradually cool. As Jeong Yeon-shin descended to the ground, the purple hem of his robe trailed behind him like a banner.
